review_title: Decent Magnesium Supplement but Didn’t Deliver Expected Benefits
review_text: I recently tried Doctor's Best High Absorption Magnesium (200 mg per serving) with the hope of improving deep sleep and alleviating foot cramps for myself and restless leg for my partner. While the product itself seems well-formulated and provides half of the daily recommended magnesium intake, we didn’t experience the benefits we were looking for. After consistent use, there was no noticeable improvement in our sleep quality or in my foot cramps. It's possible that the dosage wasn’t sufficient for our specific needs, but based on our experience, this supplement didn’t meet our expectations. If you're looking for a magnesium supplement to support sleep or muscle health, you might want to try other options on Iherb. I wouldn’t purchase Doctor's Best Magnesium again or recommend it for these specific purposes.

review_title: Possible Quality Issue with Magnesium Product
review_text: Hello! I would like to report a possible quality issue with your Magnesium product. I previously took the same supplement twice — the effect was positive: my sleep gradually improved and became deeper. After purchasing a new, larger package, the situation changed dramatically. During one week of use I developed persistent sleep disturbance: — I could fall asleep in the evening but woke up after ~2 hours — after that I was barely able to sleep (sleep lasting only 10–15 minutes) — a feeling similar to after several cups of coffee: internal stimulation and inability to fall asleep despite physical sleepiness — there were no additional factors (stress, schedule changes, etc.) After stopping magnesium, my sleep returned the very next day (light but normal for me). About ~10 days later I tried taking it again to check — and the reaction repeated exactly: a few hours of sleep followed by nighttime stimulation and inability to fall asleep. Considering that previous packages worked well, I have concerns about this particular one. I am taking the vitamins according to a doctor’s recommendation. I am ready to provide the batch number and photos of the packaging. Thank you!

Doses less than 350 mg daily are safe for most adults. In some people, magnesium might cause stomach upset, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and other side effects. High doses of magnesium from supplements or medications (not foods) can cause more severe side effects. Large doses might cause too much magnesium to build up in the body, causing serious side effects including an irregular heartbeat, low blood pressure, confusion, slowed breathing, coma, and death. In addition, the magnesium in supplements can INTERACT with some types of antibiotics and other medicines. DON'T COMBINE WITH: vitamin E, Omega, Iron, B1, Calcium (there also are recommendations to take the two strictly in 1:1 proportion, though). Magnesium might decrease blood pressure. Taking magnesium with medication for high blood pressure might cause your blood pressure to go too low. Magnesium seems to help relax muscles. Taking magnesium along with muscle relaxants can increase the risk of side effects of muscle relaxants. As for antibiotics: Aminoglycoside antibiotics can affect the muscles. Magnesium can also affect the muscles. Quinolone antibiotics: Magnesium might decrease how much antibiotic the body absorbs. Taking magnesium along with some antibiotics might decrease the effectiveness of some antibiotics. To avoid this interaction take these antibiotics at least 2 hours before, or 4 to 6 hours after, magnesium supplements. Tetracycline antibiotics: Magnesium can attach to tetracyclines in the stomach. This decreases the amount of tetracyclines that the body can absorb. Taking magnesium along with tetracyclines might decrease the effectiveness of tetracyclines. To avoid this interaction take magnesium 2 hours before or 4 hours after taking tetracyclines.) Magnesium can decrease how much bisphosphate the body absorbs. Taking magnesium along with bisphosphates can decrease the effectiveness of bisphosphate. To avoid this interaction take bisphosphonate at least two hours before magnesium or later in the day. Good news: you can take magnesium at any time of the day! We all want to buy a piece of health and beauty for a reasonable price. Just keep in mind that when taking a supplement you need to consider all side effects and drug interactions first. And come up with a regimen which avoids bad combinations, favors good ones and takes into consideration the best time of the day to take the supplement. As for oils, they also have some side effects. Before using any oil, or any cosmetic product, for the first time, you should do a patch test to make sure you're not allergic. Спасибочки! I do appreciate your “YES”! :-)

It is probably the coating, either the ""croscarmellose sodium" or the "hydroxypropyl cellulose" or both together that give me a bad reaction. Feel really weak and my legs seem to want to buckle, liver toxicity is happening in my opinion... The negative effects are mostly over after 24 hours. Positive effects are also experienced. So a bit of a difficult choice to make: do I take this or not? The package is a really good size and I do feel a deeper muscle relaxation, better sleep, so that is a benefit. Doctor`s best has mostly high quality products. Did not take it long enough to establish what it does for my joints. Not a bad taste in my experience.
